codeReview.summary: The feature adds a substantial procedural dungeon system with coverage for generation/scaling data, but two blocking gaps remain: boss/mid-boss loot guarantees are not enforced at runtime, and save/load from inside the dungeon respawns at the portal instead of the Abyss Rift entrance.

issues.description: Runtime loot generation only enforces guaranteed quality floors for monsters marked `isMiniBoss`. The dungeon final boss (`dungeon_abyss_lord`) is not marked `isMiniBoss`, and `generateLoot()` never reads `monster.lootTable`, so the promised 'final boss guarantees ≥1 rare+' and 'mid-bosses ≥1 magic+' behavior is not actually implemented in gameplay; it only exists in data/tests.

Autosaving inside the dungeon stores the player on `abyss_rift` at the dungeon portal coordinates (`60,58`), and `exitDungeon()` also returns there. The feature spec requires save/load inside the dungeon to return the player to the Abyss Rift entrance, but this implementation respawns them at the portal instead.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Monster `lootTable` entries are currently descriptive data only for most monsters; runtime drops come from `LootSystem.generateLoot()` and quality-floor helpers, not by interpreting `MonsterDefinition.lootTable` directly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/scenes/ZoneScene.ts:2452-2467 calls `lootSystem.generateLoot(monster.definition, ...)`; src/systems/LootSystem.ts:20-64 never consumes `monster.lootTable`, except indirect mini-boss handling via flags. The random-dungeons worker and tests treated lootTable as if it drove guaranteed drops.

summary: Reviewed commit f7f3278 and the worker transcript/handoff. The procedural floor generation, portal wiring, scaling, and tests are substantial, but the feature does not fully meet the spec because guaranteed boss loot is not enforced at runtime and dungeon save/load returns to the portal area rather than the Abyss Rift entrance. Review status: fail.
